payload.md:
## PR #431 rework — pick the architecture
DB-templated Email+SMS for all tenants: defaults shipped, cloneable per tenant/branch, typed `{variables}` + mandatory building blocks, translatable.

Old system (6,256-line autopsy in Obsidian `Reservine/BE/Templates/2026-07-11-PRFunctionality.md`): **3 token vocabularies** drifting, **3 interpolation engines** (real emails ship literal `{token}`), **2 SMS template systems** + duplicate DB rows, per-email cold **Node subprocess** bypassed in tests, dead NotificationDispatcher, unguarded `render`, cross-tenant `clone`.

**PHP definition classes = single source of truth.** One class per template: variables (typed enum + resolvers), blocks (required flags), per-locale defaults IN CODE. DB stores **overrides only** (`base_hash` → drift detection). Rendering = Blade email components, markdown-lite bodies. No Node anywhere, ever.

**Pros:** simplest + fastest to ship · migrations become `templates:audit` / `migrate-token` over ASTs · defaults change = code PR, zero data migration · lowest FE effort (form + simple editor) · golden-file tests in plain PHPUnit
**Cons:** throws away React Email visuals — new Blade kit needed · email-client CSS work redone · markdown-lite bodies less rich than block editor · orchestration (channel routing) stays as-is
**Risk:** Blade kit renders worse across mail clients than React Email did → mitigate with table-based reset + one Litmus pass on top clients

**Keep React Email — at BUILD time only.** CI compiles TSX layouts/blocks → HTML skeletons with named slots (versioned artifacts). Tenants edit **structured JSON block documents** (no HTML anywhere → XSS impossible by schema). Runtime = pure PHP slot filling. FE preview consumes the SAME skeletons → pixel-identical.

steps: override JSON doc? else default doc

payload.md: **Pros:** keeps the 22 polished TSX designs + dark-mode theme · best editor UX (Notion-like blocks, `if_var` conditionals kill with/without-PIN template pairs) · enum↔manifest drift = CI build error · μs renders, deterministic re-render of history
**Cons:** highest FE cost — real block editor needed · build pipeline to own (skeleton versioning, artifacts per deploy) · content = JSON docs → legacy import is lossy-ish (needs review report)
**Risk:** slot model too rigid for bespoke designs → per-template TSX layout escape hatch, still build-time

**Model notifications, not templates.** One `NotificationDefinition` per business EVENT (one enum replaces EmailType+PhoneMessagePurpose → naming fork impossible). Content in ONE layered table (`scope: default|tenant|branch`, defaults synced from repo files) resolved by ONE query. One Mustache-subset engine for subject/body/SMS. PLUS the channel orchestration + `notification_dispatches` observability log PR #431 only stubbed.

**Pros:** fixes templating AND orchestration (retires 8 hand-written jobs + both SMS systems) · full observability (every send traceable + re-renderable) · `{{#if entrance_pin}}` conditionals · WhatsApp-ready channel model · blank-body/precedence enforced by the query itself
**Cons:** biggest scope — orchestration + templating in one program · new mini-DSL (5 constructs) to spec + fuzz · default rows live in DB (synced from repo, needs verify cron)
**Risk:** big-bang feel → mitigated by per-EVENT vertical rollout (each phase deletes real legacy code); reservation family lands as one shadow-diffed release

**Hybrid sweet spot: C's orchestration + dispatch log with A's content format** (skip B's editor cost). Specs share the same definition-class/variable core by design — A can adopt C's orchestration as phase 2. Full specs: `Reservine/BE/Templates/2026-07-11-RewriteSpec-{A,B,C}-*.md`.
Pick below — answer returns to the session, then I `plan-it` the chosen spec.

questions.prompt: Which architecture for the templates rewrite?

options.label: Hybrid: C orchestration + A content
options.hint: one enum + layered table + dispatch log, but simple markdown-lite content and no block editor

options.label: A — DefinitionRegistry
options.hint: simplest, fastest, code-first defaults, Blade rendering

options.label: B — PrecompiledSkeletons
options.hint: keeps React Email visuals, JSON block docs, needs FE block editor

options.label: C — LayeredContentPipeline (full)
options.hint: biggest scope: templating + orchestration + observability in one program
